titleStmt.title: Translation of DCA DCD Misc. Ch. 564

p.seg: David king of the Scots, and Henry his son, to all his sheriffs and each of his barons, French and English, greeting.

Know that I have given and conceded herein Swinton in feu to my knight Arnulf, to him and his heir with all his men and livestock, to hold well, freely and honourably, just any of my barons best and most freely holds, and whatever pertains to it, by the same customs by which Liulf son of Edulf and Udard his son held, to hold of Saint Cutherbert and of me by rendering 40 shillings to the monks of Durham without any other services.
